judge_result.judge:
@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After filtering 2015 metadata correctly, the agent fetched only five articles (IDs 0–4) from the Mongo articles collection and then performed an inner join, yielding no matches with the 2015 article_ids (e.g., 13, 18, 26...), so the classification step saw an empty dataset and incorrectly concluded there were no World articles.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent queried the articles collection without specifying a limit, received only 5 documents, resulting in an empty join with the 2015 metadata and thus returning "Unable to determine" instead of computing the correct top region (Africa).
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After correctly joining 2015 metadata with articles, the agent’s execute_python step used a naive keyword-based classifier that failed to identify any “World” articles (defaulting many to "Unknown"), resulting in zero counts and an “Unable to determine” answer instead of selecting the top region.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After correctly retrieving 2015 article metadata and joining with article contents, the execute_python step used an overly narrow keyword-based classifier that defaulted many items to "Unknown" and resulted in zero 'World' articles, leading to no top region despite the data (which should identify Africa).
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent correctly retrieved 2015 metadata and article texts and merged them, but its ad-hoc keyword classifier (which defaults to "Business" when no keywords are found) produced zero "World" classifications, resulting in an empty group-by and a final "Unable to determine" answer instead of identifying the top region.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@
